questions.question: Which workspace setting disables the ability to share a page publicly on the web?

options.text: Disable public page sharing
options.explanation: Turning on this setting removes the "Share to web" option from every page, preventing public links.

options.text: Disable guests
options.explanation: Disabling guests blocks inviting external users, but does not affect public links for existing pages.

options.text: Disable export
options.explanation: Export controls downloading content, not the ability to view a page via a public URL.

options.text: Disable moving or duplicating pages
options.explanation: That setting stops pages from being moved to other workspaces; it does not affect public sharing.

questions.question: Which of the following SIEM platforms is listed as a native Notion integration?

options.text: Splunk
options.explanation: Splunk is explicitly mentioned as a supported SIEM integration.

options.text: Azure Sentinel
options.explanation: Azure Sentinel is not listed among the native integrations.

options.text: AWS GuardDuty
options.explanation: GuardDuty is not referenced in the article.

options.text: Google Cloud Security Command Center
options.explanation: This service is not part of the Notion SIEM integration list.

questions.question: When exporting an entire Enterprise workspace, which file formats are available?

options.text: CSV, Markdown & PDF
options.explanation: The guide states that workspace exports can be downloaded as CSV, Markdown, and PDF.

options.text: DOCX only
options.explanation: DOCX is not offered as an export format for Notion workspaces.

options.text: XLSX & PPTX
options.explanation: Spreadsheet and presentation formats are not part of the export options.

options.text: HTML & TXT
options.explanation: While HTML can be exported for individual pages, it is not listed as a workspace‑wide export format.

questions.question: How does a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) integration help protect sensitive information in Notion?

options.text: It detects sensitive data and can alert owners, redact content, or restrict page access
options.explanation: DLP tools scan for patterns (e.g., credit card numbers) and can trigger alerts, redaction, or permission changes.

options.text: It encrypts all data at rest automatically
options.explanation: Encryption is a separate security layer; DLP focuses on detection and response, not encryption.

options.text: It blocks any external sharing regardless of content type
options.explanation: DLP acts based on content classification, not a blanket block on sharing.

options.text: It creates automatic backups of every page
options.explanation: Backup creation is not a function of DLP integrations.

questions.question: Which action is NOT possible directly from the Content Search view for Enterprise workspace owners?

options.text: Export pages as PDF directly from the search results
options.explanation: Content Search provides visibility and permission editing, but does not include a one‑click PDF export feature.

options.text: View who currently has access to a page
options.explanation: The search view shows current access permissions.

options.text: Modify the permissions of a page
options.explanation: Owners can change permissions via the "Change permissions" option in the search list.

options.text: Discover pages that were abandoned by former employees
options.explanation: Search can filter by creator and status, helping locate orphaned pages.

questions.question: What primary purpose does the Audit Log serve for Enterprise workspace owners?

options.text: Provides an overview of events to aid security investigations and troubleshooting
options.explanation: The Audit Log records a wide range of actions, enabling owners to spot suspicious behavior and resolve issues.

options.text: Monitors real‑time chat messages for profanity
options.explanation: Chat monitoring is not part of the Audit Log functionality.

options.text: Automatically backs up all workspace data daily
options.explanation: Backup is handled via Workspace Exports, not the Audit Log.

options.text: Assigns user roles based on activity patterns
options.explanation: Role assignment is a separate admin task; the Audit Log is read‑only.

questions.question: If the "Disable guests" setting is turned on, how can a workspace owner still allow external collaborators?

options.text: By enabling guest invite requests for granular external access
options.explanation: Owners can toggle the option to let members send guest invite requests even when the default guest restriction is active.

options.text: They cannot; all external access is blocked
options.explanation: The guide specifies a workaround via guest invite requests.

options.text: By turning on public page sharing
options.explanation: Public sharing is a separate setting and does not grant guest invitations.

options.text: By exporting the page and sending the file externally
options.explanation: Exporting bypasses the guest‑invite workflow but does not provide collaborative editing.

questions.question: Which statement about Notion AI and customer data is accurate according to the guide?

options.text: Notion does not use customer data to train its AI models
options.explanation: The guide explicitly says that customer data is not used to train Notion AI.

options.text: Notion uses anonymized customer data for AI training
options.explanation: The article denies any use of customer data for training, even anonymized.

options.text: Customer data is sold to third‑party advertisers for AI development
options.explanation: There is no mention of selling data; the policy prohibits such use.

options.text: Customers must grant a license for their data to be used in AI training
options.explanation: The guide states the opposite: no license is granted for training.

questions.question: Which scenario best illustrates the unique protection offered by disabling "export" compared to only disabling "public page sharing"?

options.text: Even users with internal access cannot download page content as PDF, HTML, Markdown, or CSV
options.explanation: Disabling export prevents any download of content, regardless of who can view the page, whereas disabling public sharing only blocks external link access.

options.text: Pages can no longer be accessed via a public URL
options.explanation: That is the effect of disabling public page sharing, not export.

options.text: Pages cannot be moved to other workspaces
options.explanation: Moving pages is controlled by a separate setting, not export.

options.text: External guests are prevented from being invited
options.explanation: Guest invitations are governed by the "Disable guests" toggle.

questions.question: How can an organization owner manage security settings across all workspaces in an Enterprise account?

options.text: Use the workspace switcher and select "Manage organization" to adjust settings at the organization level
options.explanation: The guide notes that organization owners can control security, data, and compliance settings from the "Manage organization" view.

options.text: Edit each workspace’s security tab individually
options.explanation: While possible, it does not provide organization‑wide management.

options.text: Configure settings through a third‑party admin console
options.explanation: No third‑party console is mentioned for organization‑level control.

options.text: Organization owners cannot change security settings; only workspace owners can
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that organization owners have that capability.
